profile.name: default
profile.description: Repository default CPU strategy configuration for method reconstruction work.

results.name: Fractionation Pipeline
results.objective: Expand Bifid-style candidates through repeating-key, autokey, and periodic-transposition post layers.
results.hypothesis: K4 may require fractionation plus at least one additional keyed layer before the anchor clues emerge.
results.summary: Top candidate scored 304/1000 via bifid:CLOCK:period=11 -> direct -> post_periodic_transposition:w9:row->column.
notes: Expanded only the best direct Bifid candidates through repeating-key, autokey, and periodic-transposition post layers.

results.name: Corpus Running Key
results.objective: Slide public corpus windows across K4 and rerank the strongest clue-aligned running-key decryptions.
results.hypothesis: Public Howard Carter and official clue corpora contain usable key-stream fragments for a method-reconstruction track.
notes: Scanned public corpus windows from: official, carter.
Used anchor alignment as a fast prefilter before full corpus-aware scoring.

results.name: Periodic Transposition Hillclimb
results.objective: Search ragged periodic transposition families with keyword seeds and local permutation hillclimbs.
results.hypothesis: K4 may be hiding its anchors inside a periodic transposition that needs stronger ranking than raw brute force.
notes: Searched widths 5-32 with row/column orientations, row reversals, column reversals, and keyword-seeded permutations.
Used local swap hillclimbs instead of raw Cartesian brute force over every permutation.

results.name: Hybrid Pipeline Search
results.objective: Compose exactly two stages from fractionation, running-key, key-layer, and periodic-transposition families.
results.hypothesis: Sanborn's nested hints point to a short pipeline of interacting transforms rather than a single classical cipher.
results.summary: Top candidate scored 403/1000 via bifid:CLOCK:period=11 -> direct -> post_periodic_transposition:w9:row->column -> periodic_transposition:w27:row->column.
notes: Composed exactly two stages from fractionation, running-key, key-layer, and periodic-transposition families.
Beam width capped at 256 candidate states for this execution.
